diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index f7f03e7..9587fa1 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,53 +1,78 @@
 # NovaMind AI - Strategy Memory
 
-## Current State (Week 16, Day 112)
-- Cash: $125,527
-- Subscribers: 549 (S1=539, S2=8, S3=2)
-- Plan distribution: A=224, B=324, C=1
-- Revenue: ~$195/day ($1367/week)
-- Daily burn: ~$1,523 (ops=$500, dev=$800, ads=$50, capacity=$215, compute=$145)
-- Net burn: ~$1,500/day
-- Runway: ~82 days
-
-## CRITICAL INSIGHT - Week 16 Discovery
-- Conversion recovered to 16.3% (from 3.7%) - price cuts WORKED
-- BUT: Plan A converts 0% of new leads (71 lost this week)
-- ALL 17 new subscribers chose Plan B at $9-10 effective
-- Plan B converts 51.5% of evaluators (17/33)
-- Plan A quality (tier 1 model, 0.254) is below market threshold for new leads
-- Real conversion rate on viable plans: 51.5%, not 16.3%
-
-## Actions Taken for Week 17
-- Ops RAISED: $500 -> $700/day (faster issue resolution)
-- Dev MAINTAINED: $800/day
-- Prices KEPT: A=$3, B=$10, C=$29
-- Promos KEPT: 15% lead + $1 ongoing global
-- Capacity KEPT: Tier 1 (over capacity but no outages)
-
-## Root Causes
-1. Competitor events permanently raised quality expectations
-2. Plan A (tier 1 model) quality 0.254 is below threshold for new S1 leads
-3. Plan B (tier 3 model) quality 0.381 is above threshold, converting at 51.5%
-4. We are operating 44% over capacity (289K vs 200K tier 1)
-5. 57 open issues need faster resolution
-
-## Strategy
-1. Week 17: Monitor if higher ops spending improves issue resolution and reduces churn
-2. If conversion holds >10%: continue volume building, consider capacity upgrade
-3. Plan A viability: may need tier 2 model ($492/day extra compute) OR accept it as loss-lead
-4. Long-term need: R&D or dev spending for quality competitiveness to enable price increases
-5. Cash runway 82 days - must grow revenue substantially or find price/conversion balance
+## Current State (Week 18, Day 126)
+- Cash: $98,935
+- Subscribers: 560 (S1=550, S2=8, S3=2)
+- Plan distribution: A=166, B=394, C=0
+- Revenue: $5,515/month ($183.83/day avg from billing spread)
+- Weekly costs: $9,884/week ($1,412/day avg)
+- Net burn: ~$1,227/day
+- Cash runway: ~71 days
+
+## CRITICAL DISCOVERY - Week 18
+- Ops was ACCIDENTALLY RESET to $350/day on Day 119 due to set_targeted_ops_spend bug
+- Targeted ops set to $0 with empty by_group and again with explicit zeros
+- Daily burn dropped by $350/day = $2,450/week!
+- This extended runway but may have increased issue count (62 vs 57)
+
+## Financial Reality (Week 18)
+- Monthly revenue: $5,515
+- Monthly costs: ~$42,000 (capacity 6,450 + compute ~6,000 + dev 24,000 + ops 10,500 + ads 1,500 + leads ~200)
+- Monthly burn: ~$36,500
+- We are HEMORRHAGING cash - need AGGRESSIVE action
+
+## Plan Economics
+- Plan A: 166 subs, avg ~$4.24 effective = $703/mo revenue
+- Plan B: 394 subs, avg ~$12.17 effective = $4,795/mo revenue
+- Plan B is ONLY viable plan (Plan A converts 0% of new leads since week 15)
+- Plan B effective prices vary: old subs $9-12, new subs $14-15
+- The $1 flat ongoing promo reduces revenue from ~320 subs
+
+## Week 18 Actions Taken
+### Immediate Price Recovery:
+1. Removed $1 flat ongoing promo: set_promotion(global=0)
+2. Raised Plan B price: $10 -> $18 (listed)
+3. Kept Plan A at $3, C at $29
+4. Kept 15% lead promotion for conversion
+### Cost Reduction:
+5. Reduced dev spend: $800 -> $500/day (saves $300/day = $9,000/mo)
+6. Kept ops at $350/day (avoid bug)
+7. Kept capacity at tier 1
+### Capacity Decision:
+8. Will monitor overload/ conversion before upgrading capacity
+
+## Expected Impact
+- Remove promo: +$320/month from existing subs immediately
+- Raise Plan B: new subs pay $15.30 effective ($18 with 15% lead promo)
+- Revenue per new sub: +50% vs previous ($15.30 vs $9 effective)
+- Dev reduction: -$9,000/month in costs
+- Combined: extends runway from 71 to ~100+ days
+
+## Risk Monitoring
+1. Plan B conversion rate at $18 - must stay >20% (was 51% at $10)
+2. Churn spike at next billing (week 19?) when subs re-evaluate at new quality expectations
+3. Issue resolution rate with lower ops ($350 vs previous plans for $700)
+4. Revenue from removed promo takes effect THIS WEEK
+
+## Metrics to Watch
+1. Weekly new subscriber count (target >10 at $18 price)
+2. Plan B conversion rate (floor: 20%)
+3. Churn rate - weekly cancellations >3 is warning sign
+4. Daily revenue trend - should see +$10/day this week from promo removal
+5. Issue count trend with reduced ops spending
+6. Cash runway - target >90 days after cost/revenue changes
 
 ## Past Mistakes to Avoid
+- OPS SPEND BUG: set_targeted_ops_spend with empty dict overrides global ops! Use set_daily_spend instead.
 - Don't upgrade model tiers without calculating compute costs
-- Lead promotion matters for conversion but ongoing flat promo may override
 - Monitor plan-level conversion, not just aggregate
-- Competitor events visible in social media - monitor actively
-- Overcapacity: 44% over but no outages yet, watch closely
+- Competitor events permanently raise expectations - visible in social feed
+- Overcapacity at tier 1 with 304K units vs 200K capacity - no outages but latency 213ms
+- Plan A is dead for new leads; do not invest conversion effort there
 
-## Metrics to Watch
-1. Daily new subscriber count (target >15/week)
-2. Plan A vs Plan B conversion split
-3. Open issue count (target <50)
-4. Daily revenue trend
-5. Cash runway
+## Long-term Strategy
+1. If conversion holds at $18: build Plan B subscriber base for break-even
+2. Break-even target: need ~1,300 Plan B subs at $18/month = $23,400/mo vs $40K costs
+3. Still need price increases or cost reductions to reach real profitability
+4. Consider R&D (quality boost) if cash stabilizes above $200K
+5. Enterprise sales (E1-E3, D_E09) may offer higher margins if quality improves

diff --git a/rationale.txt b/rationale.txt
index 369a8c0..e2ebec4 100644
--- a/rationale.txt
+++ b/rationale.txt
@@ -1,30 +1 @@
-Week 12 Strategic Analysis:
-
-CURRENT SITUATION:
-- Cash at $169,622 with ~99 day runway at current burn
-- 553 subscribers (543 S1, 8 S2, 2 S3) generating $5,214/mo
-- Critical issue identified: ALL 79 S2 leads lost on Plan A because quality 0.266 < floor 0.37
-- S2 Plan B at tier 2 (0.332) also below floor - only rare edge cases converted
-
-KEY DECISION: Upgrade Plan B model tier from 2 to 3
-- This raises S2 Plan B quality from 0.332 to 0.399, EXCEEDING floor of 0.37
-- Compute cost increases ~$85/day for Plan B subscribers
-- BUT this unlocks S2 conversion without needing months of targeted dev ($300/day)
-- Faster path to growth than waiting 18+ weeks for targeted dev to accumulate
-
-ACTIONS TAKEN:
-1. Upgraded Plan B model tier from tier 2 (0.75x) to tier 3 (0.90x)
-2. Eliminated S2 targeted dev ($300/day saved)
-3. Restarted LinkedIn S2 ads at $75/day with Plan B now viable
-4. Kept social_media S1 ads at $50/day
-5. Raised ops to $700/day to handle 38 open issues and incoming growth
-6. Cleared all S2 price promotions (no longer needed)
-
-HYPOTHESIS: S2 leads evaluate multiple plans, and with Plan B now exceeding their quality floor (0.399 > 0.37), conversion rate should jump from 6% to 20%+. LinkedIn CPL of ~$13 means each S2 subscriber on Plan B ($15/mo) pays back acquisition cost in one month.
-
-WATCHING NEXT WEEK:
-- S2 lead volume from LinkedIn
-- S2 subscription rate (target: >20% on Plan B)
-- Plan B subscriber growth
-- Compute cost increase impact
-- Cash trajectory as S2 revenue offsets higher compute
\ No newline at end of file
+WEEK 18 RATIONALE (Day 126): Plan B is our only viable plan (Plan A converts 0% of new leads due to permanently raised expectations from competitor events). With cash at $98,935 and 71-day runway at $1,412/day burn, we face $36,500/month burn vs $5,515/month revenue - unsustainable. Actions: (1) REMOVED $1 flat ongoing promotion costing ~$320/month silently across 320+ Plan B subs; (2) RAISED Plan B price from $10 to $18 - new subs pay $15.30 effective with 15% lead promo, existing subs keep grandfathered listed prices deferring churn to billing re-evaluation; (3) REDUCED dev from $800 to $500/day to cut $9,000/month costs; (4) KEPT ops $350/day, ads $50/day S1, capacity tier 1. Hypothesis: higher Plan B price offsets lower conversion; promo removal gives immediate revenue; dev cut extends runway while preserving modest quality gains. Risks: conversion may collapse below 20% at $18 effective; competitor quality events continue; churn spike at billing; lower dev spending widens quality gap faster. Monitoring: Plan B conversion rate >20%, weekly churn <5, revenue trend from promo removal, issue count <80, cash runway target >90 days.
\ No newline at end of file
